mnemonics.title: sigaction Flags Memory
mnemonics.content: R-S-N-R: Restart, Siginfo, Nodefer, Resethand. Think 'Really Sad Nodes Require Restarts' - but replace 'Sad' with 'Signal' to remember: Really Signal Nodes Require Restarts.

mnemonics.title: siginfo_t Fields
mnemonics.content: P.I.N. = Process ID Number - si_pid, si_signo, si_uid. Add 'Code' for si_code. Remember: 'Please Identify Number - Code Included'.

mnemonics.title: sigaction Structure
mnemonics.content: HMS: Handler (sa_handler) Mask (sa_mask) Settings (sa_flags). Remember 'His Majesty Serves' - Handler, Mask, Settings.

acronyms.usage: Automatically restarts interrupted system calls like read(), write(), open() when signal handler returns.
Enables extended signal handler with access to siginfo_t containing sender PID, UID, and additional context.
One-time handler that automatically resets to default action (SIG_DFL) after being executed once.
Allows the signal being handled to interrupt its own handler (does not auto-block the delivering signal).

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box divided into 4 sections: top-left sa_handler (function pointer), top-right sa_sigaction (extended handler), bottom-left sa_mask (signal set), bottom-right sa_flags (options). Label it like a building floor plan.
Draw: Signal Source → Kernel → Process → Signal Handler Execution. Show sa_mask blocking other signals during handler, and SA_RESTART looping back system calls.

Two parallel boxes: Simple handler (sa_handler: receives only signal number) vs Extended handler (sa_sigaction: receives signal number + siginfo_t + context). Show SA_SIGINFO arrow pointing to extended version.

analogies.analogy: signal() is like a simple doorbell with one tune, while sigaction() is like a programmable doorbell where you can choose the tune, volume, and what happens when someone rings.
Think of a signal handler as an important meeting. sa_mask is like putting a 'Do Not Disturb' sign on your door during the meeting - certain signals (people) cannot interrupt.
SA_RESTART is like an automatic reconnect feature. If your call drops (signal interrupts), it automatically dials back (restarts the system call) instead of leaving you disconnected.
SA_RESETHAND is like a fire alarm that automatically resets after going off - it works once, then returns to normal until manually reset again.
